summary: Verify compile help text after update

content.text:
Compile one or more agentic workflows to YAML workflows.

If no workflows are specified, all Markdown files in .github/workflows will be compiled.

The workflow-id is the basename of the Markdown file without the .md extension.
You can provide either the workflow-id (e.g., 'ci-doctor') or the full filename (e.g., 'ci-doctor.md').

The --dependabot flag generates dependency manifests when dependencies are detected:
  - For npm: Creates package.json and package-lock.json (requires npm in PATH)
  - For Python: Creates requirements.txt for pip packages
  - For Go: Creates go.mod for go install/get packages
  - Creates .github/dependabot.yml with all detected ecosystems
  - Use --force to overwrite existing dependabot.yml
  - Cannot be used with specific workflow files or custom --dir
  - Only processes workflows in the default .github/workflows directory

Examples:
  gh aw compile                    # Compile all Markdown files
  gh aw compile ci-doctor          # Compile a specific workflow
  gh aw compile ci-doctor daily-plan  # Compile multiple workflows
  gh aw compile workflow.md        # Compile by file path
  gh aw compile --dir custom/workflows  # Compile from custom directory
  gh aw compile --watch ci-doctor     # Watch and auto-compile
  gh aw compile --trial --logical-repo owner/repo  # Compile for trial mode
  gh aw compile --dependabot        # Generate Dependabot manifests
  gh aw compile --dependabot --force  # Force overwrite existing dependabot.yml

Usage:
  gh aw compile [workflow]... [flags]

Flags:
      --action-mode string          Action script inlining mode (inline, dev, release). Auto-detected if not specified
      --action-tag string           Override action SHA or tag for actions/setup (overrides action-mode to release). Accepts full SHA or tag name
      --actionlint                  Run actionlint linter on generated .lock.yml files
      --actions-repo string         Override the external actions repository used in action mode (default: github/gh-aw-actions)
      --dependabot                  Generate dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod) and Dependabot config when dependencies are detected
  -d, --dir string                  Workflow directory (default: .github/workflows)
  -e, --engine string               Override AI engine (claude, codex, copilot, custom)
      --fail-fast                   Stop at the first validation error instead of collecting all errors
      --fix                         Apply automatic codemod fixes to workflows before compiling
      --force                       Force overwrite of existing dependency files (e.g., dependabot.yml)
      --force-refresh-action-pins   Force refresh of action pins by clearing the cache and resolving all action SHAs from GitHub API
  -h, --help                        Show help for gh aw compile
  -j, --json                        Output results in JSON format
      --logical-repo string         Repository to simulate workflow execution against (for trial mode)
      --no-check-update             Skip checking for gh-aw updates
      --no-emit                     Validate workflow without generating lock files
      --poutine                     Run poutine security scanner on generated .lock.yml files
      --purge                       Delete .lock.yml files that were not regenerated during compilation (only when no specific files are specified)
      --refresh-stop-time           Force regeneration of stop-after times instead of preserving existing values from lock files
      --runner-guard                Run runner-guard taint analysis scanner on generated .lock.yml files (uses Docker image ghcr.io/vigilant-llc/runner-guard:latest)
      --safe-update                 Force-enable safe update mode independently of strict mode. Safe update mode is normally equivalent to strict mode: it emits a warning prompt when compilations introduce new restricted secrets or unapproved action additions/removals not present in the existing gh-aw-manifest. Use this flag to enable safe update enforcement on a workflow that has strict: false in its frontmatter
      --schedule-seed string        Override the repository slug (owner/repo) used as seed for fuzzy schedule scattering (e.g. 'github/gh-aw'). Bypasses git remote detection entirely. Use this when your git remote is not named 'origin' and you have multiple remotes configured
      --stats                       Display statistics table sorted by workflow file size (shows jobs, steps, scripts, and shells)
      --strict                      Override frontmatter to enforce strict mode validation for all workflows (enforces action pinning, network config, safe-outputs, refuses write permissions and deprecated fields). Note: Workflows default to strict mode unless frontmatter sets strict: false
      --trial                       Enable trial mode compilation (modifies workflows for trial execution)
      --validate                    Enable GitHub Actions workflow schema validation and action SHA validation
  -w, --watch                       Watch for changes to workflow files and recompile automatically
      --zizmor                      Run zizmor security scanner on generated .lock.yml files

Global Flags:
      --banner    Display ASCII logo banner with purple GitHub color theme
  -v, --verbose   Enable verbose output showing detailed information

content.text:
Implemented the minimal non-test changes for the issue.

What changed:
- Removed compile-time container image validation from `gh aw compile --validate` in:
  - `pkg/workflow/compiler.go`
- Updated `--validate` help text so it no longer claims to perform container image validation:
  - `cmd/gh-aw/main.go`

Result:
- `gh aw compile --validate` no longer calls Docker for container-based MCP tools.
- No Docker warning is emitted when Docker is unavailable.
- Runtime behavior is left unchanged; container pull/accessibility failures remain runtime concerns.

Verification performed:
- Created a temporary reproducer at `/tmp/gh-aw-docker-validate-repro.sh`.
  - Pre-fix manual reproduction confirmed `docker info` was invoked and emitted the reported warning.
  - Post-fix run with two container workflows, including an unpullable image, passed with:
    - `0 warning(s)`
    - no fake Docker invocations
- Ran targeted tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v -run 'TestValidateDockerImage_RejectsHyphenPrefix|TestCompilerAcceptsExplicitBashSyntax|TestCompileWorkflowWithRefresh|TestCompileWorkflowActionReferences' ./pkg/workflow ./pkg/cli`
  - Passed.
- Ran command package tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v ./cmd/gh-aw`
  - Passed.
- Ran existing container validation integration tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v -tags integration -run 'TestCompileWithInvalidContainer|TestValidateContainerImages|TestValidateDockerImage' ./pkg/workflow`
  - Passed/skipped appropriately because Docker is not installed.
- Confirmed no test files were modified.

Notes:
- A full `go test -v ./pkg/workflow` run still fails, but the failures are unrelated to this change (`TestGitPatchFromHEADCommits`, `TestMCPGSupportsIntegrityReactions`, and wasm golden tests). Targeted tests covering the modified paths pass.
- Pre-existing untracked files `=` and `test-results.json` were left untouched.

Please continue working on the task on whatever approach you think is suitable.
When you think you have solved the question, please use the finish tool and include your final answer in the message parameter of the finish tool.
IMPORTANT: YOU SHOULD NEVER ASK FOR HUMAN HELP.

content.text:
package cli

import (
        "context"

        "github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/constants"
        "github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/logger"
        "github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

var validateLog = logger.New("cli:validate_command")

// NewValidateCommand creates the validate command
func NewValidateCommand(validateEngine func(string) error) *cobra.Command {
        cmd := &cobra.Command{
                Use:   "validate [workflow]...",
                Short: "Validate agentic workflows without generating lock files",
                Long: `Validate one or more agentic workflows by compiling and running all linters without
generating lock files. This is equivalent to:

  gh aw compile --validate --no-emit --zizmor --actionlint --poutine

If no workflows are specified, all Markdown files in .github/workflows will be validated.

` + WorkflowIDExplanation + `

Examples:
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate                         # Validate all workflows
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate ci-doctor               # Validate a specific workflow
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate ci-doctor daily         # Validate multiple workflows
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate workflow.md             # Validate by file path
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate --dir custom/workflows  # Validate from custom directory
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate --json                  # Output results in JSON format
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate --strict                # Enforce strict mode validation
  ` + string(constants.CLIExtensionPrefix) + ` validate --fail-fast             # Stop at the first error`,
                RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
                        engineOverride, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("engine")
                        dir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("dir")
                        strict, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("strict")
                        jsonOutput, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
                        failFast, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("fail-fast")
                        stats, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("stats")
                        noCheckUpdate, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check-update")
                        verbose, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("verbose")

                        if err := validateEngine(engineOverride); err != nil {
                                return err
                        }

                        // Check for updates (non-blocking, runs once per day)
                        CheckForUpdatesAsync(cmd.Context(), noCheckUpdate, verbose)

                        validateLog.Printf("Running validate command: workflows=%v, dir=%s", args, dir)

                        config := CompileConfig{
                                MarkdownFiles:  args,
                                Verbose:        verbose,
                                EngineOverride: engineOverride,
                                Validate:       true,
                                NoEmit:         true,
                                Zizmor:         true,
                                Actionlint:     true,
                                Poutine:        true,
                                WorkflowDir:    dir,
                                Strict:         strict,
                                JSONOutput:     jsonOutput,
                                FailFast:       failFast,
                                Stats:          stats,
                        }
                        if _, err := CompileWorkflows(context.Background(), config); err != nil {
                                return err
                        }
                        return nil
                },
        }

        cmd.Flags().StringP("engine", "e", "", "Override AI engine (claude, codex, copilot, custom)")
        cmd.Flags().StringP("dir", "d", "", "Workflow directory (default: .github/workflows)")
        cmd.Flags().Bool("strict", false, "Override frontmatter to enforce strict mode validation for all workflows (enforces action pinning, network config, safe-outputs, refuses write permissions and deprecated fields). Note: Workflows default to strict mode unless frontmatter sets strict: false")
        cmd.Flags().BoolP("json", "j", false, "Output results in JSON format")
        cmd.Flags().Bool("fail-fast", false, "Stop at the first validation error instead of collecting all errors")
        cmd.Flags().Bool("stats", false, "Display statistics table sorted by workflow file size (shows jobs, steps, scripts, and shells)")
        cmd.Flags().Bool("no-check-update", false, "Skip checking for gh-aw updates")

        // Register completions
        cmd.ValidArgsFunction = CompleteWorkflowNames
        RegisterEngineFlagCompletion(cmd)
        RegisterDirFlagCompletion(cmd, "dir")

        return cmd
}

This would make the detection job resilient regardless of artifact contents. action.message:
Implemented the requested change to remove Docker as a dependency of `gh aw compile --validate`.

Changes made:
- Removed the compile-time container image validation call from `pkg/workflow/compiler.go`.
  - `--validate` no longer calls `validateContainerImages(...)`.
  - This prevents Docker CLI/daemon checks and image pulls during compilation.
- Updated the `--validate` flag help text in `cmd/gh-aw/main.go`.
  - It now says: `Enable GitHub Actions workflow schema validation and action SHA validation`.

content.text:
Implemented the requested change to remove Docker as a dependency of `gh aw compile --validate`.

Changes made:
- Removed the compile-time container image validation call from `pkg/workflow/compiler.go`.
  - `--validate` no longer calls `validateContainerImages(...)`.
  - This prevents Docker CLI/daemon checks and image pulls during compilation.
- Updated the `--validate` flag help text in `cmd/gh-aw/main.go`.
  - It now says: `Enable GitHub Actions workflow schema validation and action SHA validation`.
  - It no longer advertises container image validation as part of `--validate`.

Verification performed:
- Created a temporary reproduction script at `/tmp/gh-aw-docker-validate-repro.sh` using a fake `docker` binary.
- Verified post-fix behavior with two container-based MCP workflows, including an intentionally unpullable image:
  - `gh-aw compile --validate --no-emit` completed successfully.
  - No Docker commands were invoked.
  - No `container image validation failed` or `could not validate container image` warnings were emitted.
- Ran targeted tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v -run 'TestValidateDockerImage_RejectsHyphenPrefix|TestCompilerAcceptsExplicitBashSyntax|TestCompileWorkflowWithRefresh|TestCompileWorkflowActionReferences' ./pkg/workflow ./pkg/cli`
  - Passed.
- Ran command package tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v ./cmd/gh-aw`
  - Passed.
- Ran CLI compile-specific tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v -run 'TestCompile' ./pkg/cli`
  - Passed.
- Ran existing container validation integration tests:
  - `TMPDIR=/workspace/gh-aw/tmp/test-tmp go test -v -tags integration -run 'TestCompileWithInvalidContainer|TestValidateContainerImages|TestValidateDockerImage' ./pkg/workflow`
  - Passed/skipped appropriately because Docker is not installed.

Also confirmed:
- No test files were modified.
- Only these source files changed:
  - `cmd/gh-aw/main.go`
  - `pkg/workflow/compiler.go`

Note: A full `go test -v ./pkg/workflow` run still has unrelated pre-existing failures in tests such as `TestGitPatchFromHEADCommits`, `TestMCPGSupportsIntegrityReactions`, and wasm golden tests. The targeted tests covering this change pass.
